macro_analyst:United_States:macro_indica.content: US growth remains constructive with real GDP tracking 2.3% and breadth improving across consumer and industrial activity. Valuation metrics at -1.00 versus history reflect modest overvaluation relative to long-run averages but well within the historical range for an expansionary regime. Quality factor leadership intact; momentum bid into health care and select tech.

macro_analyst:Japan:tactical.content: Japan macro tactical view stays constructive. BoJ holding pattern supports a positive regional tilt; corporate-governance reforms continue to surface value in mid-cap industrials. Recommended overweight to Japan equity with quality bias.

macro_analyst:China:macro_indicators.content: China macro picture remains soft. Property-sector drag persists; consumer confidence below trend. Underweight stance appropriate while authorities continue piecemeal stimulus.

macro_analyst:United_States:sectors.content: Tech sector showing valuation compression with Quality factor effectiveness at 0.50 — leadership rotating toward Health Care defensives and selective Industrials. Underweight tech remains the consensus position for the next quarter.
